action.reasoning: Observation: Previous training with 500 samples gave near-zero IoU; need more data and robust training. Iter 13/14 failed with vision encoder shape errors due to improper batching. Analysis: The manual training loop with collate_fn that stacks pixel_values causes shape mismatches when images have varying grid sizes. Switching to batch_size=1 with gradient accumulation avoids these issues. Also, loading model directly to cuda without device_map prevents StopIteration dtype errors. Decision: Write new train.py with full training set (7100 samples), batch_size=1, grad_accumulation=8, LoRA r=16, train for 3 epochs, log metrics, evaluate val IoU periodically, merge and save best model, then submit eval.
